title: amauropelta rivularioides
intro: Amauropelta rivularioides, also known as the river fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Amauropelta rivularioides include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and itching. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the fronds in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the leaves to be used in capsules or tinctures.
